title: Argentinian Empanadas
description: Traditional Argentinian empanadas filled with a savory beef mixture and cheese, perfect for a savory snack or light meal.
instructions: In a large pan, cook ground beef over medium-high heat, breaking it into small pieces as it cooks.
Once the beef is browned, add the diced onion, cibola, and garlic. Cook until the vegetables are softened.
Add the Worcestershire sauce and Argentinian seasoning (if using) and stir to combine.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked beef mixture, shredded cheese, and a pinch of salt (if using). Mix well.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Use a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles of dough.
Place a tablespoon or two of the beef and cheese mixture in the center of each dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with the beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape, pressing the edges together to seal the empanada.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with the remaining egg wash.
Bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
